patterns: 4 MCP tools form full agent loop: get_rules -> plan_context -> append_intent -> update_registry
Synchronous readFileSync on hot path (every tool call)
JsonlEventStore for MCP event replay (O(n) full scan)
SSE event system separate from MCP transport (chokidar + debounce)
Compliance audit trail with 5-min cross-reference window
L0/L1/L2 rules layering with scope_glob minimatch
Hash-based revision tracking for stale detection
Multi-client MCP configuration (7 AI host types)

perspectives.name: Technical
findings: KF-001: readAgentsMeta() uses synchronous readFileSync on every tool call — blocks event loop, no caching. Minimum 2x per edit cycle, potentially 3x.
KF-002: audit.jsonl grows unbounded, fully re-read on every fab_append_intent — O(n) linear scan per affected_path.
KF-003: loadGetRulesContext() executes 3 I/O ops per call (meta sync + AGENTS.md async + human-lock async + K hash reads). No cross-call caching.
KF-004: JsonlEventStore.replayEventsAfter() does O(n) full file scan on every MCP reconnect.
KF-005: SSE /events endpoint uses native EventSource which cannot send Authorization headers — breaks authenticated deployments.
KF-006: Incremental ledger byte-offset reader (readLedgerAppendedEvents) is well-designed — audit-log and JsonlEventStore should adopt same pattern.
insights: Per-tool-call I/O budget: fab_get_rules = 1 sync + 2+ async + N rule file reads + 1 audit write
10-file edit cycle = minimum 30 redundant reads of 3 stable files
SSE total latency floor: ~195ms (120ms awaitWriteFinish + 75ms debounce)
questions: Is the synchronous readFileSync in meta-reader.ts intentional for simplicity, or a known bottleneck?

code_anchors.significance: Synchronous readFileSync — single most impactful bottleneck
loadGetRulesContext() — 3 I/O ops with no caching
Full audit.jsonl re-read on every append_intent
Incremental byte-offset reader — best I/O pattern in codebase

call_chains.chain: tools/get-rules.ts -> services/get-rules.ts::getRules() -> meta-reader.ts::readAgentsMeta() [sync] + services/get-rules.ts::loadGetRulesContext() [3 I/O] -> resolveRulesForPath() [N file reads] -> audit-log.ts::appendGetRulesAuditEvent()
tools/append-intent.ts -> services/append-intent.ts::appendIntent() -> audit-log.ts::appendEditIntentAuditEvents() [full audit.jsonl read per affected_path]
POST /mcp -> http.ts::createSession() -> index.ts::createFabricServer() [new McpServer + 4 tool registrations] + new StreamableHTTPServerTransport + JsonlEventStore.replayEventsAfter() [O(n)]
chokidar file watch [120ms awaitWriteFinish] -> events.ts debounce [75ms] -> broadcast to <=10 SSE clients -> dashboard use-events.ts EventSource

perspectives.name: Architectural
findings: KF-01: CLI->Server boundary is clean (4 explicit params) but dashboardDistPath/dev options not exposed through CLI.
KF-02: process.env.FABRIC_PROJECT_ROOT is global mutation — breaks concurrent server instances, violates explicit-dependency principle.
KF-03: One McpServer per HTTP session — O(n) tool registrations, no shared instance with per-request context.
KF-04: JsonlEventStore shares ledger file path with intent entries — mixed concerns in one file.
KF-05: SSE event loss on reconnect — no Last-Event-ID replay, events during disconnect permanently lost.
KF-06: Dashboard defines parallel TypeScript types instead of importing from shared — two sources of truth for API response surface.
KF-07: /mcp endpoint has no bearer auth even when FABRIC_AUTH_TOKEN is set — asymmetric security surface.
insights: Clean separation between CLI/Server/Dashboard/Shared packages, but coupling leaks through process.env
MCP Host config detection is convention-based (filesystem probes), no runtime confirmation
Extension seam for new tools is open (add tool registration in createFabricServer), but new transports require http.ts changes
questions: Should /mcp endpoint be covered by bearer auth?
Is the per-session McpServer pattern a deliberate isolation choice?

code_anchors.significance: Parallel type definitions — drift risk with shared

call_chains.chain: cli/commands/serve.ts::run() [parse args + validateHost + readAuthToken] -> server/index.ts::startHttpServer() [dynamic import http.ts] -> http.ts::createFabricHttpApp() [set process.env, mount routes, create sessions Map]
cli/commands/init.ts -> config/resolver.ts::resolveClients() [7 filesystem probes] -> config/json.ts::writeJsonClientConfig() [merge mcpServers entry]

perspectives.name: Business
findings: F1: fab init aborts entirely if AGENTS.md exists without --force — no guidance on re-initialization.
F2: 8 visible + 3 hidden commands. No update/upgrade command for refreshing MCP configs after version upgrade.
F3: fab_get_rules re-reads 3 files per call with no cache. 10-file edit = 30 redundant reads — directly impacts user-perceived agent latency.
F4: Dashboard drift-check nav item is visible but non-interactive (muted span, no explanation).
F5: SSE disconnection shows 'connecting' indefinitely — no retry button, timeout message, or server status indication.
F6: serve command silently downgrades non-loopback host without FABRIC_AUTH_TOKEN — user may not notice.
F7: Gemini CLI detection via GEMINI.md presence is fragile — false positives possible.
F8: pre-commit runs all 3 checks serially with no fast-path for commits touching no fabric-managed files.
insights: Onboarding is well-designed (one-command setup) but has a sharp abort edge
Daily workflow overhead is dominated by uncached file reads in MCP tools
Dashboard has 5 views but incomplete polish (dead nav item, no disconnect UX)
questions: Is an update/upgrade command planned for post-install MCP config refresh?
Should pre-commit skip all checks when no fabric-managed files are in the commit?

code_anchors.significance: initFabric() — aborts entirely on existing files without --force

call_chains.chain: cli/commands/init.ts::run() -> initFabric() [4 files + 3 config files] -> bootstrapStage() -> mcpInstallStage() [resolveClients + writeConfig per client] -> hooksStage() [mergeClaudeStopHook]

perspectives.name: Domain Expert
findings: KF-01: MANDATORY directive overuse — fab_update_registry uses MANDATORY but is situationally required. Signal dilution risk.
KF-02: No outputSchema on any of 4 tools — agents receive untyped JSON blobs, must parse blindly. Highest-impact conformance gap.
KF-03: enableJsonResponse:true eliminates all streaming — fab_plan_context with large paths arrays cannot emit progress.
KF-04: Compliance score (compliant:boolean) stored in audit.jsonl but never returned to agent — audited in secret, cannot self-correct.
KF-05: AGENTS.md (L0) embedded in every tool response — should be MCP Resource with subscribe:true for agent-side caching.
KF-06: JsonlEventStore O(n) without TTL — unbounded growth.
KF-07: fab_plan_context paths.min(1) creates semantic overlap with fab_get_rules at n=1.
KF-08: fab_update_registry data field is z.record(z.unknown()).optional() — fully untyped at protocol boundary.
insights: Server is conformant with MCP 2024-11-05 core spec; all gaps are in best-practice areas from 2025-03-26+ revisions
Compliance audit pattern is elegant and novel — but one-way observation mirror (agents audited but never informed)
Only tools primitive used — resources, prompts, sampling could each add value
questions: Should compliance results be returned to the agent in fab_append_intent response?
Is there a plan to adopt MCP resources for AGENTS.md and rule files?

code_anchors.significance: No outputSchema, no annotations, generic path string type
z.record(z.unknown()) — untyped data at MCP boundary
paths.min(1) — overlap with fab_get_rules at n=1
Internal Zod schema reused as MCP tool contract — coupling risk

call_chains.chain: Agent calls fab_get_rules [audit event logged] -> Agent modifies files -> Agent calls fab_append_intent [cross-references audit within 5-min window -> sets compliant:boolean in audit.jsonl] -> compliance result NOT returned to agent

findings: MCP 2025-06-18 adds outputSchema for structuredContent responses — current tools return text-wrapped JSON
MCP resources with subscribe:true enables agent-side caching — eliminates redundant AGENTS.md reads
Missing notifications/tools/list_changed after sync-meta — agent uses stale tool list
SSE Last-Event-ID spec requires client to persist and resend — current dashboard drops it
enableJsonResponse:true trades streaming for simplicity — correct for current tool latencies
best_practices: Tool descriptions with imperative MANDATORY phrasing for enforcing call order
outputSchema reduces LLM token waste by providing type hints
Static context should be MCP resources, not embedded in tool responses
SSE reconnect must use Last-Event-ID to prevent event loss

codebase_gaps.current: Re-read AGENTS.md on every tool call
Agent discovers workflow via tool descriptions only
codebase_gaps.recommended: Expose workflow templates as MCP prompts

convergent_themes: readAgentsMeta() synchronous hot path is the #1 performance bottleneck — all 4 perspectives agree (Technical: I/O blocking, Architectural: coupling smell, Business: user latency, Domain: protocol inefficiency)
SSE event loss on reconnect (no Last-Event-ID) — confirmed by Technical, Architectural, Business, and Research
/mcp endpoint unauthenticated — Architectural and Technical both flag this as a security gap
audit.jsonl unbounded growth with O(n) full reads — Technical and Domain Expert convergent
No outputSchema on any tool — Domain Expert and Research both identify as highest-impact MCP conformance gap
Missing MCP resources primitive for AGENTS.md/rule files — Domain Expert and Research convergent
conflicting_views: Technical ranks readFileSync as #1 priority; Domain Expert ranks missing outputSchema higher — both valid, different impact dimensions (I/O latency vs token waste)
Business sees init abort UX as top friction; other perspectives focus on runtime performance — both valid for different user moments
unique_contributions: Technical: Precise I/O budget per tool call (1 sync + 2+ async + N rule reads + 1 audit write); incremental byte-offset reader as positive pattern to replicate
Architectural: /mcp auth gap, dashboard parallel types drift risk, CLI->Server option exposure gap (dashboardDistPath/dev not CLI-accessible)
Business: Missing update/upgrade command, dead drift-check nav item, pre-commit serial overhead for non-fabric commits, Gemini CLI false-positive detection
Domain Expert: MANDATORY overuse dilution, compliance score invisible to agents (one-way audit mirror), fab_plan_context/fab_get_rules overlap at n=1, untyped fab_update_registry data field
